name: Mercury
description: Mercury is the smallest and closest planet to the Sun. It experiences extreme temperature variations due to its thin atmosphere.
fact: A year on Mercury lasts only 88 Earth days.

name: Venus
description: Venus is the hottest planet in our Solar System due to its thick carbon dioxide atmosphere and runaway greenhouse effect.
fact: A day on Venus is longer than its year.

name: Earth
description: Earth is the only known planet to support life and contains vast oceans, diverse ecosystems, and a protective atmosphere.
fact: 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water.

name: Mars
description: Mars is known as the Red Planet because of iron oxide on its surface. It is a primary target for future human exploration.
fact: Mars hosts Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the Solar System.

name: Jupiter
description: Jupiter is the largest planet in the Solar System and is famous for the Great Red Spot, a giant storm larger than Earth.
fact: Jupiter's magnetic field is 14 times stronger than Earth's.

name: Saturn
description: Saturn is best known for its spectacular ring system composed of ice, rock, and dust particles.
fact: Saturn is so light that it could float in water if there were a large enough ocean.

name: Uranus
description: Uranus rotates on its side, making it unique among the planets. It appears blue-green due to methane in its atmosphere.
fact: Uranus experiences extreme seasons that last over 20 Earth years.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune is the farthest major planet from the Sun and has the strongest winds recorded in the Solar System.
